workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Can you work with me on migration architecture for Contoso Ltd?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 16:20 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 09:09 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 15:32 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Contoso Ltd

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-E trap (Opportunity hypothesis from a non-commercial inquiry); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-E failure mode (Opportunity hypothesis from a non-commercial inquiry)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-E -- Opportunity hypothesis from a non-commercial inquiry
